title: Child constipation (starter evidence summary)
text:
Child constipation is common but phone triage must identify severe illness or obstruction.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Child is very ill-appearing, difficult to wake, or has severe symptoms
- Black/tarry stools or large bleeding

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Vomiting with abdominal swelling or inability to keep fluids down

Supportive measures (if mild):
- Hydration and diet adjustments; consider pediatric clinician guidance for laxatives.
- Seek in-person care if pain, vomiting, fever, or worsening symptoms develop.
